sprint: Zero-Holder Scenes -- the real white-box source found and fixed
task: A direct, confirmed follow-up to the previous fix, found by reproducing the user's actual live World shape end-to-end rather than guessing further: a zero-Place Scene with a Scene-hosted dark Colour-fill background and a Free-hosted gold octagon Decoration Shape still showed a white box in Creator. The previous fix (closing _resolveBorder's legacy-fallback gap) was real and necessary but insufficient on its own -- it correctly made the resolved border null for a zero-Holder Scene, but render()'s own composition-selection code then routed into its 'else' branch precisely because the border was null, and that branch unconditionally paints the classic pre-Artwork-Theme 'story picture panel' rectangle (the theme's plain page colour) at the panel rect, with zero awareness of Holder count -- a leftover from before the Scene/Place/Holder system existed, correct for a plain Story Theme with no Artwork Theme, wrong for a zero-Holder Artwork Theme Scene which has no picture area to paint a background for at all. Fixed with a third branch inserted between the Quote check and the border check: when the active Layout's holders count is explicitly 0, draw nothing there at all, beyond the Scene's own slide-scoped Layer Pack content. Verified with a real git stash-based A/B test reproducing the user's exact World shape (real ProjectCompiler.runBuild, real ThemeEngine import/apply, real SlideRenderer.render): pre-fix the panel-rect center pixel reads pure white; post-fix it reads the theme's own cream page colour -- the exact bug signature confirmed present before this change and gone after it, same input, only this one code path toggled. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite (Museum Gallery, a real Holder-bearing theme, still renders its Frame/mat/Wax Seal/placeholder exactly as before) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
